rationale_for_baseline_tier: Symmetric MCP/PIP/MTP synovitis with a positive squeeze test and no single hot joint favours an RA flare, but a low-grade fever in a patient on hydroxychloroquine mandates infection exclusion before steroid escalation, and the joints cannot be examined remotely. Urgent same-day in-person review with an infection screen (T3), plus fall-safety for painful MTP weight-bearing, is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for a single fiery joint or fever >38.5 with rigors.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single joint becoming fiery red/hugely swollen, or fever above 38.5 with shaking chills
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicate septic arthritis or systemic sepsis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one joint turns fiery red and hugely swollen, or your fever climbs above 38.5 with shaking chills, go to ED or call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A fall, or being unable to mobilise/transfer safely even with help
escalation_edges.rationale: High fall risk with functional immobility warrants timely support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you fall or can't move safely even with help, arrange to be seen the same day — call an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse present and anxious, assisting with mobility
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use the spouse for fall-safety, monitoring and to enable the in-person review; provide reassurance.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Seropositive RA on hydroxychloroquine (immunomodulated) with fever
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold to exclude infection before steroids.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a flare of your rheumatoid arthritis after your cold — both hands and feet are affected symmetrically. Because you have a fever and take an immune-modifying medicine, you need an in-person review today to check your joints and rule out infection before any steroids. Keep taking your hydroxychloroquine, use simple painkillers, and stay safe from falls with your frame and supportive shoes.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person GP review with an infection screen; use ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use ED/000 for a single fiery joint or fever >38.5 with rigors; otherwise urgent same-day review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the symmetric RA flare after a viral illness, low-grade fever on hydroxychloroquine, negative septic screen so far, and the need for a joint exam/infection screen before steroids.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Prescribing steroids remotely or reassuring without an infection screen/joint review in a febrile immunomodulated patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A masked infection/septic joint worsens after blind steroids.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for a symmetric non-septic flare when urgent same-day in-person review (T3) suffices.
